pct.title: Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT)
pct.description: File a single international patent application to seek protection in multiple countries.

1.title: Log in
details: Login to the SAIP platform, select Services, and click on Patent Service.

2.title: Select PCT application
details: Navigate to Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) section.
Select the type of PCT application you want to file.

3.title: Prepare application documents
details: Prepare the request form, description, claims, abstract, and drawings.
Ensure all documents meet PCT requirements.

5.title: Pay fees and receive confirmation
details: Pay the required PCT filing fees.
Receive your international application number and filing date.

requirements: Valid patent application or priority claim
Description of the invention in Arabic or English
Claims defining the scope of protection
Drawings (if necessary for understanding the invention)
Power of attorney (if using an agent)

digitalGuide._TODO: Content below should be migrated to Drupal CMS for easier editing

about.title: About IP Rights

ipSystems.title: IP systems
ipSystems.intro: IP systems regulate a broad range of activities, including:
ipSystems.summary: These activities are indicators of intangible (non-material) efforts, intellectual innovation, and human creativity.

about.conclusion: Therefore, IP rights govern the creation, use, and investment of creative mental effort.

characteristics.title: Characteristics of IP rights
characteristics.p1: IP rights are similar to ownership rights, as they relate to controlling specific uses of intangible assets. These rights are granted when the "thing of value" can be attributed to a single inventor or group of inventors, as long as the inventor deserves recognition.
characteristics.p2: The inventor is the rightful owner, unless the financial rights are transferred to another party through mutual agreement. These rights are enforceable through civil and criminal protection methods.

scopeOfProtection.title: What exactly will be protected?

ipSystemsProperty.title: IP systems and property rights
ipSystemsProperty.p1: IP systems address all rights considered a type of property right.

scopeOfProtection.distinction: It is essential to specify the "thing" that is the subject of this property. The significance lies in the distinction between IP and traditional property, which involves tangible physical objects.

artistic.label: Artistic or literary work:
artistic.description: The property rights are located in the work itself.

patent.label: Patent:
patent.description: The property rights are in the creative idea solving a specific technical issue.

trademark.label: Trademark:
trademark.description: Ownership rights to a trademark that distinguishes goods or services.

design.label: Design:
design.description: The property rights are in the arrangement of lines, shapes, colors, or sculptures that give the product its distinct and attractive appearance.

innovators.title: Rights of innovators
innovators.p1: Innovators have the right to use the "thing" that is the subject of their IP. They also have the ability to control and exploit it. The duration of IP rights varies according to the specific system in place.

importance.title: Importance of protecting an IP
importance.intro: An effective protection system guarantees creators a level of protection, allowing them to freely use and exploit their IP. Without this system, creators would lack motivation to innovate.

publicProtection.title: Protecting the public
publicProtection.p1: Effective protection for producers of intellectual works ensures public benefits. By preventing the copying or imitation of IP, creators, and owners are incentivized, improving public welfare in various areas.

economicImpact.title: Economic impact
economicImpact.p1: A system that protects IP rights boosts employment and economic returns. Countries with strong innovation, patents, and trademarks attract both domestic and foreign investment. The protection system also attracts well-known international trademarks and makes the country a regional hub for businesses.

innovative.title: Protecting your innovative idea
innovative.p1: You may have an innovative idea, be developing or improving an existing product or service, or the result of a project with a partner, but it may still be in its early stages.

howTo.title: How to protect your idea
howTo.p1: To protect your idea, you need to develop, express, or embody it in a protectable manner, such as a patent, design, trademark, or copyright, or by using other IP tools.

guidance.title: Guidance on IP protection
guidance.p1: You can obtain guidance on protecting IP through IP clinics.

ipCategory.title: IP Category
ipCategory.selectArea: Select an area of IP that interests you.

whatIsInvention.title: What is an invention?
whatIsInvention.p1: An invention is an idea conceived by an inventor and solves a problem in technology. It must be new, innovative, and novel, improving products or methods.

whatIsPatent.title: What is a patent?
whatIsPatent.p1: A patent is a certificate granting exclusive rights to an inventor. It prevents production, use, distribution, or sale of the invention without permission for commercial purposes.

conditions.title: Conditions for obtaining a patent
conditions.noveltyDesc: The invention must be new, not already existing, and distinct from current technologies. It must not have been disclosed to the public.
conditions.inventiveDesc: The invention should involve an inventive activity, meaning, it is not obvious to skilled persons in the field. Industrial applicability: The invention must have practical or industrial use, not purely theoretical.
conditions.eligibilityDesc: The patent's subject must not violate Islamic law, harm health, or the environment, ensuring it is suitable for patenting.

cta.text: Ensure your invention is novel, inventive, and industrially applicable for patent protection.

nonDisclosure.title: Importance of non-disclosure before registration
nonDisclosure.p1: To protect a patent, avoid disclosing your invention before registration. When discussing it, share only basic information to explain the idea, without revealing details. Public disclosure before filing negatively affects novelty and patent eligibility.

nda.title: Using NDAs for protection
nda.p1: When presenting to investors or seeking funding, you may need to discuss details. Use a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) to safeguard your idea before sharing it with contractors or partners.

filing.title: Filing a patent application
filing.p1: You can file a patent application yourself through the authority's unified services platform on their website. Alternatively, appoint an accredited IP agent to file on your behalf.

drafting.title: Drafting a patent application
drafting.p1: To draft your application, search for relevant patents in databases as reference models. This approach ensures proper structure and compliance with patent requirements.

definition.title: Definition and purpose of a prototype
definition.p1: The first prototype is a preliminary version of an invention or product, used to evaluate the idea and guide development toward the final version. It transforms a theoretical idea into a tangible model or design that demonstrates basic specifications.

requirements.title: Prototypes and patent requirements
requirements.p1: While a prototype is not required to obtain a patent, it serves as a valuable tool for evaluating and refining ideas before market launch. Developing a prototype can also assist in presentations to investors.

guidance.title: Guidance for patent preparation
guidance.p1: Leverage the IP Clinics for assistance in preparing and filing a patent with the authority. These services provide support throughout the process.

disclosure.title: Full disclosure requirement for applications
disclosure.p1: The application must include a detailed description of the invention, enabling any skilled professional familiar with the field to implement and apply it. This principle ensures knowledge exchange between the inventor and society.

period.title: Patent protection period
period.p1: In exchange for disclosing the invention, the inventor receives 20 years of non-renewable protection. After this period, the invention becomes public property, allowing unrestricted use by all.

details.title: Providing invention details
details.p1: Inventors must include all invention details in the application. Policies and guidelines are available to assist in drafting forms and submitting them to SAIP.

platform.text: Start the patent application process on the SAIP Platform

journey.text: Interested in understanding patents better? Start your journey here!

whatIs.title: What is design?
whatIs.p1: Design refers to the decorative or aesthetic aspect of a product, which is not for functional or technical purposes. It can be three-dimensional (e.g., product shape) or two-dimensional (e.g., patterns, lines, colors), including textile designs.

protection.title: Design protection
protection.p1: A design protection document grants the owner exclusive rights to use, license, or sell the design and prohibits copying or imitation without permission. Registering a product's shape as a design protects its external appearance, boosting its commercial value and marketability.

commercial.title: Commercial use and restrictions
commercial.p1: Exploitation of the design for commercial purposes must not violate Islamic law, harm living beings, or damage the environment. The design must also avoid including trademarks, flags, or official logos belonging to others.

cta.text: Check the uniqueness and aesthetic appeal of your design to secure its registration.

protection.title: Designs Protection

conditions.title: Industrial model conditions for mandatory availability
items: The industrial model must be new, and its quality standard should not be disclosed to the public through publication, use, or any other means before the date of filing the registration or priority request.
It must have distinctive features that set it apart from known models.

searching.title: Searching for new designs
searching.p1: To verify the novelty of a design, you can search specialized databases. These databases help identify new product ideas, improve existing products, and ensure no infringement on the IP rights of others.

protection.p2: You can search registered design information using keywords, competitor names, designers, or by using international classes and categories based on the Locarno classification for designs.

prototype.title: The prototype

model.title: The prototype model
model.p1: The initial prototype model is a preliminary version of a product or invention, used as a basis for evaluating the idea and development towards the final product intended for market release. It is designed to transform a theoretical idea into a tangible product or practical application model, or a design model to display the basic specifications.

importance.title: Importance of the prototype
importance.p1: While presenting an initial model is not required to obtain a design certificate, it is a valuable tool for evaluating and refining ideas before launching the final product in the market.

prototype.p2: Additionally, developing an initial experimental model may be helpful for presentation to investors.

clinics.title: IP Clinics
clinics.p1: You can benefit from guidance services provided by IP clinics.

registration.title: Registration of designs

submitting.title: Submitting a design application
submitting.p1: You can submit an electronic application for registering an design through the Unified Platform for IP Services on the SAIP website if your place of residence is within Saudi Arabia.

registration.p2: Alternatively, you can appoint an authorized IP agent to submit the application on your behalf.

reference.title: Searching for reference models
reference.p1: To submit the application for registering the design yourself, you can search for relevant design certificates in the previously mentioned databases and use them as a reference model when preparing your application.

platform.text: Start the design registration process on the SAIP Platform

journey.text: Interested in understanding designs better? Start your journey here!

notEligible.title: The idea is not eligible for IP protection.
notEligible.description: It must be documented, created, or developed into a tangible form.

notIP.title: Probably your idea is not an IP.
notIP.description: For more information and help go to IP Clinics.

articlesPolicies.pageDescription: Learn about SAIP's Intellectual Property Blog policies and submission guidelines
articlesPolicies.title: Articles polices
articlesPolicies.blogDescription: The Intellectual Property Blog is an Arabic-language platform launched by the Saudi Authority for Intellectual Property (SAIP). It is dedicated to publishing short articles on topics related to intellectual property. The blog aims to enhance Arabic content by discussing and addressing various intellectual property issues and themes.
publishingGuidelines: The article must be original and not extracted from previously published material, even by the same author.
The article should be concise (600–1200 words), divided into coherent paragraphs. Short or excessively long articles will not be accepted unless the length is necessary for content clarity.
The content must be relevant to intellectual property topics.
The article should not be descriptive in nature.
Key points should be presented in the introduction, clearly highlighting the problem being addressed.
The article should discuss the problem, analyze it, and conclude with a proposed solution.
References and sources must be reliable, with hyperlinks included where applicable. Direct quotes should be enclosed in quotation marks.

Intellectual property rights must be respected, avoiding any infringement of third-party rights. Authors are fully responsible for any violations, and SAIP reserves the right to delete articles without prior notice if such infringements occur.
submissionInstructions: Articles should be submitted in a Word file, including the author's full name in Arabic at the end, along with a brief biography and email address.
Articles or inquiries should be sent to the blog's email: rs.policies@saip.gov.sa.
The blog team will review submissions for idea clarity and language accuracy, ensuring adherence to the blog’s quality standards.
If all conditions are met, the article will be published directly without requiring revisions, and the author's details will be acknowledged upon publication.
Articles that do not meet the blog’s requirements will be rejected outright without notifying the author.
Once published on the blog, the article cannot be republished or reused elsewhere without prior approval from SAIP.

gazette.heading: IP Gazette
gazette.text:
The IP Gazette is your trusted source for all trademark-related updates. Here, you will find details of trademark applications submitted on or after 19/12/2023, along with any subsequent changes to these records.

Whether it's renewals, ownership transfers, or modifications, the Gazette ensures you stay informed with the latest and most accurate trademark information.

copyrights.heroSecondSubheading: An author is the creator of a work, identified by their name unless stated otherwise. For anonymous or pseudonymous works, the publisher may represent the author. This term also includes contributors like scriptwriters and dialogue authors in visual or audio projects.

designs.heroSubheading: Industrial designs protect the visual appearance of products, enhancing their marketability without affecting their function.
designs.overviewHeaderDescription: Watch the video and learn the key steps involved in designs.
designs.publicationsDescription: The design publications provides important updates and information on design procedures, changes in regulations, and relevant industry developments in Saudi Arabia.

services.title: Design Certificate Renewal
services.description: Renew your design protection certificate to maintain exclusive rights over your registered design.

services.title: Design Search
services.description: Search existing design registrations to ensure your design is novel and doesn't infringe on existing rights.

journey.description: We are dedicated to empowering design holders by providing a seamless and transparent experience throughout the design protection process.
journey.secondDescription: From fostering innovation to protecting, managing, and enforcing your design rights, we ensure your creations are safeguarded and transformed into valuable assets that enhance market presence and drive economic growth. Learn more about the design journey.
